creator: Better Cheat Meals 🇳🇿 | Steve Kemp
source_text:
Kansas City-Style BBQ Chicken Bites & Crispy Potatoes 🔽

452 Cal | 48g P | 45g Carb | 6g F

👉 Stay on track with 110+ quick & satisfying digital recipes, link in bio 📱

Served in 2½ cup (600 ml) glass containers

Servings: 4

🔥 Ingredients
Chicken:
-1¾ lb (800 g) chicken breast cut into 1-inch pieces
-olive oil spray
-2 lbs (1 kg) lotatoes/Carisma potatoes low-carb potatoes, cut into small pieces

BBQ-Style Coating:
-½ cup (60 g) all-purpose flour
-2 tbsp (16 g) cornstarch
-1 tsp baking powder
-1 tsp salt
-1 tsp garlic powder
-1 tsp onion powder
-3 tsp paprika or smoked paprika
-1 tsp chili powder
-1 tsp ground black pepper

BBQ Sauce:
-⅓ cup (70 g) replacement brown sugar (monk fruit)
-⅓ cup (80 ml) low-sugar ketchup
-1½ tsp red wine vinegar
-½ tsp Worcestershire sauce
-1½ tsp dry mustard
-¾ tsp paprika
-¾ tsp salt
-½ tsp pepper
-dash hot sauce

Instructions 
-Preheat oven to 400 F (200 C), prep 2 baking sheets with parchment 
-Dice chicken into 1-in (2.5 cm) pieces 
-Cut potatoes into cubes

Baking: 
-Toss potatoes with oil spray, salt, pepper; spread on sheet 
-Bake 25-30 mins, flip halfway until crispy 
-Mix chicken and coating in a sealed container 
-Arrange on second sheet, bake 15-20 mins until internal temp of 165 F (74 C)

Sauce: 
-Combine sauce ingredients in saucepan 
-Simmer on medium heat to blend flavors 

Assembly: 
-Layer potatoes in bowls 
-Top with BBQ chicken 
-Drizzle sauce, garnish with green onions

Storage & Reheating 
Fridge: Chicken & potatoes 3-4 days, sauce 1 week 
Freezer: Up to 3 months 

Reheat: Oven 350 F (180 C) 12-15 mins covered, or microwave 2-3 mins
Notes 
-For low-carb: Use Lotatoes/Carisma potatoes or swap for cauliflower 
-If using regular potatoes, reduce to 1 lb (453g) for similar macros

#mealprep #weightloss #fatloss
